scope: TG-P5 generated G5 8B attention vs owned HIP (in-model W==D + route attribution + token match)
decision: REFUTE: keep owned HIP as the 8B attention default; generated G5 route is correct + route-bound but slower

gates.microgate: PASS (generated G5 token-identical to owned oracle at ctx512 and ctx4096)
gates.route_bound: PASS (generated route fires flash_block_tiled_xlane_score_pv_tile_whole_cache_32_128; owned tile does not fire under the candidate)
gates.resource_gate: PASS (K_ONLY staging ~4KB LDS per GP3; 3-kernel flash split, no spill observed)
gates.wd: REFUTE (87.6% of owned @ctx512, 95.6% @ctx4096 — below the not-slower tolerance)
gates.rollback: n/a (owned remains default; generated route is the opt-in, DECODE_FLASH_BLOCK_TILE_G5_8B / policy)
gates.census: UNCHANGED (decode_attention_owned_two_kernel remains the default; external_handwritten purity debt persists)

stop_rule_applied: TG-P5 stop rule: generated attention correct but slower -> keep owned HIP default, do not force purity by slowing the model
implication_for_north_star: TINYGRAD_DEFAULT_PURITY_PASS (0 external_handwritten defaults) is NOT achievable for the 8B attention without a decode slowdown; the owned two-kernel tile is the honest remaining purity debt
